knowledge.html:
I. PRONUNCIATION (PHÁT ÂM)

1. Quy tắc phát âm của các chữ cái phụ âm và phụ âm cuối

Một số chữ cái phụ âm thường gặp

Chữ cái phụ âmCách phát âmVí dụ
c/k/close, confide
/s/certify, cycle
/ʃ/special, ocean
d/d/dramatic, demand
/dʒ/gradual, educate
g/g/guess, regular
/dʒ/germ, origin
/ʒ/beige, garage
n/n/neck, fun
/ŋ/uncle, drink
s/s/secret, optimist
/z/rose, resume
/ʃ/sugar, ensure
/ʒ/usually, occasion
t/t/tutor, pretence
/ʃ/option, initial
/tʃ/culture, question
x/gz/exist, exhibit
/ks/box, mixture
/kʃ/anxious, luxury

Một số nhóm chữ cái phụ âm thường gặp

Nhóm chữ cái phụ âmCách phát âmVí dụ
qu/kw/queue, require
ch/k/chemist, mechanic
/tʃ/check, bunch
/ʃ/machine, parachute
sh/ʃ/shock, smash
th/θ/theme, depth
/ð/thus, feather
gh/ph/f/rough, lâughter, photo, paragraph

2. Cách phát âm đuôi -s, -es

AmQuy tắc
/s/Sau các am vô thanh: /p/, /t/, /k/, /f/, /θ/
Ex: cloths, beliefs, books, cups, cats
/iz/Sau các am: /s/, /z/, /ʃ/, /ʒ/, /tʃ/, /dʒ/ (sáng chiều giò xô xó)
Ex: crashes, watches, buzzes, boxes, buses, focuses, resources, bridges
/z/Sau các am hữu thanh còn lại
Ex: robs, bags, pools, costumes, begins, floors, leaves

3. Cách phát âm đuôi -ed

AmQuy tắc
/id/Sau am /t/ va /d/
Ex: wanted, ended
Ngoại lệ: Đuôi "ed" trong các tính từ sau được đọc la /id/: naked, aged, learned, wicked, dogged, blessed, beloved, crooked
/t/Sau các am vô thanh: /k/, /ks/, /s/, /ʃ/, /tʃ/, /p/, /f/, /θ/
Ex: worked, kissed, faxed, watched, laughed, faced, helped, roughed, washed
Lưu ý: -s hoặc -ss thi luôn đúng, -se có thể doc /t/ hoặc /d/ tùy theo từ.
/d/Sau các am hữu thanh còn lại
Ex: played, loved, happened

4. Quy tắc phát âm các tu chua âm câm

Một số chữ cái trong một số tu bao gồm phụ âm va nguyên âm không được phát âm thanh tieng, được gọi la âm câm (silent sounds).

Chữ cái - Trường hợp thường gặpVí dụ
b dùng cuối trong một số tu (thuong đi sau m)climb, dumb, comb, doubt, debt
b dùng trước tsnack, dock
c dùng sau s trong một số tuscene, muscle, Wednesday
d trong một số tuhandsome, Wednesday
h trong một số tuhour, exhausted
gh trong một số tu (đặc biệt la sau i)weigh, sight
k dùng trước nknow, knee, knife
l trong một số tuhalf, could
n dùng sau mautumn, condemn
p dùng đầu một tu, theo sau là một phụ âm va một số trường hợp khácpsychology, receipt
r dùng trước một phụ âm khác hoặc dùng cuối tu docard, park, farm, burn, neighbour, volunteer
t trong một số tulisten, castle
w dùng trước r hoặc h trong một số tuwreck, who

exercises.question: Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently from the others.
exercises.explanation: 'Sure' starts with /ʃ/ sound, while the others start with /s/. The 's' in 'sure' is pronounced like 'sh'.

exercises.question: Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently from the others.
exercises.explanation: 'Document' has /ɒ/ for 'o', while stroll /əʊ/, control /əʊ/, remote /əʊ/ all have the long /əʊ/ sound.

exercises.question: Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently from the others.
exercises.explanation: 'Average' has /æ/ for 'a', while 'wave', 'pavement', 'range' have /eɪ/. The 'a' in 'average' is the odd one out.

exercises.question: Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently from the others.
exercises.explanation: 'But' has /ʌ/ for 'u', while sugar /ʊ/, put /ʊ/, full /ʊ/ all have the short /ʊ/ sound.

exercises.question: Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently from the others.
exercises.explanation: 'Considered' ends in /d/ sound (voiced ending before -ed). The others end in /t/ because they follow voiceless consonants. 'Considered' = /d/, rest = /t/.

exercises.question: Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently from the others.
exercises.explanation: 'Leather' has /e/ for 'ea', while stream, cream, dream all have /iː/. Think: leather = /leðə/, but dream = /driːm/.

exercises.question: Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently from the others.
exercises.explanation: 'Medicine' has /e/ (short) for the first 'e', while the others have /iː/ (long e). Medicine = /ˈmedsɪn/, reason = /ˈriːzən/, immediate = /ɪˈmiːdiət/, medium = /ˈmiːdiəm/.

exercises.question: Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently from the others.
exercises.explanation: 'Bury' is pronounced /ˈberi/ with /e/ sound, while hurry, hungry, thunder all have /ʌ/. Bury is a tricky word!

exercises.question: Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently from the others.
exercises.explanation: 'Worked' has -ed pronounced /t/ (after voiceless /k/). The others have /ɪd/ because they end in /t/ or /d/: planted, wanted, needed.

exercises.question: Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently from the others.
exercises.explanation: 'Instead' has /e/ for 'ea', while beach, clean, leave all have /iː/. Instead = /ɪnˈsted/.

exercises.question: Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently from the others.
exercises.explanation: 'Impressed' has -ed = /t/ (after voiceless /s/). The others have -ed = /d/ (after voiced sounds): informed, installed, admired.

exercises.question: Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently from the others.
exercises.explanation: 'Nature' has /eɪ/ for 'a', while planet, fashion, travel all have /æ/. Nature = /ˈneɪtʃə/.

exercises.question: Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently from the others.
exercises.explanation: 'Donate' has /əʊ/ for 'o', while hut, done, tunnel all have /ʌ/ sound. Donate = /dəʊˈneɪt/.

exercises.question: Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently from the others.
exercises.explanation: 'Parachute' has 'ch' = /ʃ/, while chemistry, mechanic, character all have 'ch' = /k/. These are Greek-origin words.

exercises.question: Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently from the others.
exercises.explanation: 'Reduced' has -ed = /t/ (after voiceless /s/). The others have -ed = /d/ (after voiced sounds): realized, arrived, compared.

exercises.question: Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently from the others.
exercises.explanation: 'Leather' has 'ea' = /e/, while breathe, decrease, beach all have 'ea' = /iː/. Leather = /ˈleðə/.

exercises.question: Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently from the others.
exercises.explanation: 'Cow' has 'ow' = /aʊ/, while mow, low, show all have /əʊ/. Think: cow rhymes with 'how', but the others rhyme with 'go'.

exercises.question: Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently from the others.
exercises.explanation: 'Speaks' has -s = /s/ (after voiceless /k/). The others have -s = /z/ (after voiced sounds): learns, sends, knows.

exercises.question: Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently from the others.
exercises.explanation: 'Patient' has 'ti' = /ʃ/ (before -ent), while invite, faint, victim all have /t/. Patient = /ˈpeɪʃənt/.

exercises.question: Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently from the others.
exercises.explanation: 'Award' has 'a' = /ə/ (schwa), while candidate, understand, examine all have 'a' = /æ/. Award = /əˈwɔːd/.
